channel.title: 👨‍💻 UM BSc Computer Science
channel.description: Last 10 notes on 👨‍💻 UM BSc Computer Science

item.title: BSc Computer Science
item.description: First year core courses § Concepts IP Address Transmission Control Protocol Uniform Resource Locator Period 1 BCS1110 Introduction to computer science BCS1120 Procedural programming BCS1130 Discrete mathematics BCS1300 Project 1-1 Period 2 BCS1220 Objects in programming BCS1440 Calculus .

item.title: IP Address
item.description: The ISP provides the IP Addresses → 4 byte (4 * 8 bits = 32 bits) address IPv4 and IPv6 standards Static IP → IP Address does not change (e.g. for web services) Dynamic IP → IP addresses change frequently (e.

item.title: Transmission Control Protocol
item.description: Functionality Separates information into packets Sends and reassembles packets Ensures information is complete and correct Packet → data parcel sent across a network .

item.title: Uniform Resource Locator
item.description: Protocol → Rules for the information Domain name → human readable, gets converted to an IP Address via a Domain Name Server IP Address → Computer readable unique address for a machine in a network Hierarchy → each byte of the address gets more specific .

item.title: BCS1110 Introduction to computer science
item.description: Course description § None Recommended reading § “Computational Thinking for the Modern Problem Solver” by David Riley, Kenny A. Hunt “Computer Science Illuminated” by Nell B.

item.title: BCS1110-1 Intro lecture
item.description: Lecture notes § Standardisation vs Non-Standardisation George Polya’s problem solving approach Lecture 1 tutorial § Recommended readings § Nine algorithms that changed the future by John MacCormick .

item.title: BCS1110-10 Exam Prep
item.description: Lecture notes Lecture 1 (Introduction) Lecture 2 (Hardware) Lecture 3 & 4 Lecture 5 Lecture 6 Lecture 7 Lecture 8 Lecture 9 Demo exam paper Key takeaways Lecture slides Lecture notes § Lecture 1 (Introduction) § BCS1110-1 Intro lecture Is computer science a science? Engineering? Why? Read Required Reading → Why social aspects matter in computer science problems HTML example of creativity and logic intertwined in computer science Standardization & Non-Standardization → e.

item.title: BCS1110-2 Computer Hardware
item.description: Exam info Most important is: Binary number system (and conversion) What CPU is and how it works Basic computer components Lecture notes § Initial terms § Transistors → Can amplify or switch eletronic signals and eletrical power.

item.title: BCS1110-4 Command Line and Git
item.description: Lecture notes § Version control and Git § Functions of version control Saves versions Assigns unique names to each version Maintains the relations between the various versions Keeps track of the current version pointer Most common actions in version control Commit / Checkin → Current version gets updated with the changes Dropping a version / Reverting back → move the current version back to a previous commit Master/Main branch → “Official” version Repository → Structured library of a version control mechanism (local or online) Checking out → Retrieving a read-only copy of the code Merge → Conflict → Issues in merging commits.
